messages.content: rewrite: Mackenzie is currently receiving 16 required credit hours out of 22.  She is currently taking courses in Edgenuity, which  are  standard-based online learning resource for school districts produced by Imagine Learning, which teaches kindergarten through 12th-grade in core, elective, credit recovery, technical, and career subjects, through both remedial and accelerated work.

Mackenzie is currently enrolled in 16 out of 22 credit hours, which are being completed through Edgenuity. Edgenuity is an online learning platform created by Imagine Learning, designed to provide comprehensive education from kindergarten to 12th grade. They offer a wide range of courses, including core subjects, electives, credit recovery, technical training, and career-focused subjects. The platform allows students to work at their own pace, offering both remedial and accelerated options.

rewrite: As of August 30, 2023, Mackenzie teachers has observed her in a classroom setting.  Their results are as follows:
CTE Teacher: Mackenzie is off to a great start in Economics and Personal Finance, period one.  She has completed and turned in all preliminary course documents and told me today that she looking forward to this new school year.  She has that SENIOR glow.

Sped Teacher:  "She seems very determined to do well in school and interested in her career after H.S."

On August 30, 2023, Mackenzie's teachers provided their observations of her performance in the classroom:

According to the CTE (Career and Technical Education) teacher, Mackenzie has a solid beginning in Economics and Personal Finance in the first period. She has demonstrated commitment by completing and submitting all initial course materials. Today, Mackenzie expressed her enthusiasm for the upcoming school year, exhibiting a confident and positive attitude expected of a senior.

The SPED (Special Education) teacher remarked that Mackenzie showcases a strong drive to excel academically and shows genuine interest in her future career beyond high school.

rewrite: Mackenzie has relative strengths in math and receptive vocabulary in the average range according to her Wechsler Individual Achievement Test.  She has performed a passing rate in her algebra I standards of learning assessment.

According to the Wechsler Individual Achievement Test, Mackenzie demonstrates solid abilities in math and has a receptive vocabulary within the average range. Additionally, she has achieved a passing score on her Algebra I Standards of Learning assessment.
rewrite: Mackenzie is able to communicate her needs and wants effectively in the classroom and amongst her peers.
Mackenzie possesses effective communication skills, both in expressing her needs and desires within the classroom and among her peers.
rewrite: mackenzie's disability does not affect her communication and/or speech skills.
Mackenzie's disability has no impact on her ability to communicate and speak effectively.
Mackenzie home/independent living skills are age appropriate.
Mackenzie demonstrates age-appropriate skills in home and independent living.

rewrite: Although we have only been in school less than a week, no data is present to determine a need as of date.  Mackenzie's prior reports suggest increase attendance in class and time management skills.

Since the school year has just begun, there is currently insufficient data available to assess any specific needs as of the given date. However, Mackenzie's previous reports indicate a potential for improvement in attendance and time management skills.
rewrite: When comfortable Mackenzie, will ask for assistance, and is interested in having a successful year by increasing attendance.
Once Mackenzie feels comfortable, she actively seeks assistance when needed and shows a genuine interest in making the current academic year a success by focusing on improving her attendance.
mackenzie is able to participate in community activities
Mackenzie actively engages in various community activities.
